topic.title: HTML Elements, Tags & Attributes
topic.description: HTML elements, tags, and attributes are the core building blocks used to structure web content.

text.value: is the complete unit: opening tag, content, and closing tag. A
is just the angle-bracket notation like
lives inside the opening tag and provides extra information about the element. The complete reference for all HTML elements is maintained at

rows: Self-closing, no content or closing tag
Name-value pair inside the opening tag
Presence alone means true, no value needed

content.title: Elements vs Tags
content.text:
Developers often say tag when they mean element. Technically a tag is just the

or

bracket notation. The full element includes everything between and including the opening and closing tags.

examples.title: Anatomy of an HTML Element
examples.description: This shows the four parts of every HTML element: opening tag, attributes, content, and closing tag. It also covers void elements that have no closing tag, and boolean attributes that are toggled by presence alone.
examples.code_snippet:
This is a paragraph element.

This paragraph has bold text and italic text inside it.

Card content
examples.output: The page renders: a paragraph with class and id, a paragraph containing bold and italic text, a broken image placeholder (photo.jpg does not exist locally), a text input, a horizontal rule, a greyed-out disabled Submit button, and a div with text 'Card content'. The disabled button appears greyed out because of the disabled attribute.

examples.title: Boolean Attributes and Data Attributes
examples.description: Boolean attributes are turned on by their presence and turned off by removing them entirely. Data attributes store custom information on elements that JavaScript can read. Both patterns are shown here in the simplest possible form.
examples.code_snippet:
Remember me
Wireless Earbuds — Rs 1,499
examples.output: The page shows a required email input, a disabled text input pre-filled with 'johndoe', a pre-checked checkbox labelled 'Remember me', a horizontal rule, a div with text 'Wireless Earbuds — Rs 1,499', and a button 'Show Product Info'. Clicking the button fills the paragraph below it with: 'ID: SKU-4821 | Price: Rs 1499 | In Stock: true'.

Use the CSS attribute selector with data attributes to apply styles based on state without adding extra classes. For example:
applies error styles. This keeps your styling tied to meaningful data rather than arbitrary class names, making the intent clear in both HTML and CSS.
selects all elements that have a
attribute regardless of its value. This pattern lets you declaratively mark elements in HTML and initialize them all with a single JS selector rather than assigning individual IDs or classes.

subtopics.title: Global and Boolean Attributes
subtopics.description: Global attributes apply to all HTML elements and boolean attributes toggle on by their presence.

text.value: Global attributes are a set of attributes accepted by every HTML element without exception. Key ones include
are toggled on by their presence and toggled off only by removing them entirely. Full list at

rows: Unique identifier for JS selection and fragment links
One or more CSS class names for styling and JS hooks
Declares language for this element and its children
Removes element from layout and accessibility tree
Controls keyboard tab order (use only 0 or -1)
Makes element content editable in the browser
Blocks interaction and excludes from form submission
Prevents form submission if field is empty
Pre-selects the input on page load
Allows selecting more than one option or file
Shows value but prevents editing by the user

examples.title: Global Attributes and Boolean Attribute Toggling
examples.description: This shows the most commonly used global attributes on different elements, and the correct way to toggle boolean attributes on and off using JavaScript. It also shows the most common mistake — setting a boolean attribute to the string 'false'.
examples.code_snippet:
Every HTML element accepts global attributes.

Welcome. यहाँ हिंदी सामग्री है।

This paragraph is hidden and takes up no space.

Tab to focus this element
Remember me
examples.output: The page shows: a paragraph with a hover tooltip, a paragraph with a Hindi span, a hidden paragraph that appears after 2 seconds, a keyboard-focusable div, a required email input, a disabled text input pre-filled with 'johndoe', a pre-checked checkbox, a greyed-out disabled Save button, and an Enable/Disable toggle button. Clicking the toggle alternately enables and disables the Save button.

subtopics.title: Custom Data Attributes
subtopics.description: Custom data attributes store private metadata on HTML elements, readable by JavaScript via dataset.

text.value: Custom data attributes follow the syntax
where * is any lowercase name you choose. They store structured data directly on elements so JavaScript can read it without separate API calls or global variables. The browser's

content.title: Security Warning
content.text: Data attributes are fully visible in browser DevTools to any user. Never store passwords, API tokens, session IDs, or any sensitive user data in data- attributes. Use server-side sessions or secure HTTP-only cookies for private data.

examples.title: Reading and Writing Data Attributes with JavaScript
examples.description: This shows the three things you need to know about data attributes: how to write them on an element in HTML, how to read them in JavaScript using dataset, and how to update them. The kebab-case to camelCase conversion is also demonstrated.
examples.code_snippet: Mechanical Keyboard — Rs 2,499
examples.output: The page shows a div with product text, a 'Read Data' button, a 'Buy One' button, and an empty paragraph. Clicking 'Read Data' fills the paragraph with: 'ID: PRD-881 | Price: Rs 2499 | Stock: 5 | In Stock: true'. Clicking 'Buy One' decrements the stock count and shows 'Bought one! Remaining: 4'. After 5 clicks it shows 'Out of stock!'.

subtopics.title: HTML Div and Span Elements
subtopics.description: Div and span are generic containers used only when no semantic HTML element fits the content needed.

text.value: generic container. It starts on a new line and takes the full available width. The

generic container. It flows within text without breaking the line. Neither element has semantic meaning. They exist purely for styling hooks and JavaScript targets when no suitable semantic element exists. Always prefer

content.title: When to Use div and span
content.text: Use div when you need a block container purely for layout or styling and no semantic element fits. Use span when you need to target a portion of inline text with CSS or JavaScript and no semantic inline element like strong, em, or code fits.

examples.title: Div and Span vs Semantic Elements
examples.description: This shows when div and span are the right choice, and when they should be replaced by a semantic element. The rule is simple: if a semantic element exists that describes the content's meaning, use that instead.

examples.output: The page renders: a card grid div containing two product cards with headings and price paragraphs. The wrong div-heavy blog section renders plain text blocks. The semantic article section renders a heading and a clickable Home link. The inline comparison shows pairs of identical-looking text — the semantic versions carry meaning for screen readers and search engines even though they look the same visually.

text.value: of HTML. When you have a specific idea, you write it on a labelled card:

is the same as the difference between a

in a sentence. Div is a paragraph break: it starts a new block, takes the full width, and pushes content before and after onto separate lines. Span is a word: it flows inline within surrounding text without disrupting the line.
When you find yourself writing class names like
, that is a signal to replace each div with its semantic HTML equivalent:
. Semantic elements are free built-in labels that give your structure meaning without any extra code.

seo_metadata.meta_title: HTML Elements, Tags & Attributes | Global, Boolean, Data Attributes Guide
seo_metadata.meta_description: Learn HTML elements, tags, and attributes: global attributes, boolean attributes, custom data attributes, and div vs span with production examples and best practices.
